title: Spike: information-driven bars for XAU (tick / tick-imbalance) — quantify vs D1
source: LdP Ch2 information-driven-bars investigation (3 parallel read-only agents: MT5 tick feasibility, AFML Ch2 construction, pipeline integration impact), 2026-06-03. Conversation: investigar tick/volume bars antes de implementar features.
why: AFML Ch2 argues time bars (D1/H4) are statistically inferior (oversample quiet periods; serial correlation, heteroscedasticity, non-normal returns) and recommends activity-clock bars built from TICK data. Investigation verdict for XAU SPOT CFD: only TICK BARS and TICK-IMBALANCE BARS (TIB) are genuine — MT5 ticks are bid/ask QUOTE updates with last~=0 and real volume~=0, so VOLUME bars degenerate to tick bars and DOLLAR bars / VIB / DIB are infeasible (no real traded volume). The IID-Normal benefit comes from the activity clock itself (tick / signed-tick count), which we CAN capture (tier-1 of AFML benefit); the volume/dollar-weighting gains (Clark 1973, dollar-bar count stability) are UNREACHABLE here. CRITICAL CAVEAT: MT5 tick history is shallow (weeks-months on the demo broker, NOT the ~20y of D1 bars) — which collides head-on with the documented fold-starvation/data-scarcity bottleneck (MEMORY stack_gate_starvation): cleaner bars but far less calendar history = likely FEWER usable events.
value_rationale: Honest M: a worthwhile spike to quantify the distributional improvement, NOT a clear win. The no-real-volume ceiling (tick bars only) + shallow tick history (worsens the binding data constraint) cap the upside. Measure before committing the L-effort bar layer.
scope: Pull a SAMPLE of XAU tick history via mt5.copy_ticks_range (net-new; no script uses copy_ticks today) — windowed/paged; expect bid/ask quotes, last~=0, volume~=0
Build tick bars (N quote-updates) + TIB (signed by tick rule on mid-price) — AFML Ch2 §2.3.1/§2.3.2; no reference code exists, transcribe from book prose
MEASURE vs D1: return normality (e.g. Jarque-Bera), serial correlation, heteroscedasticity — does the activity clock deliver the claimed improvement on XAU quote data?
Quantify the history tradeoff: how many events/regimes does the available tick window yield vs 20y D1? Does it worsen fold-starvation?
Decision gate: only build the full bar layer if the spike shows a real distributional gain AND enough history
recommendation: Run as a bounded SPIKE first. Pipeline is mostly bar-agnostic (triple-barrier=bars, WF=event-index, Sharpe annualization=calendar-span — all survive unchanged); real rework if it graduates: bar-construction layer (L), fix features.py rv sqrt(252)->empirical bars_per_year (M), recalibrate rolling windows (M), validate macro daily-.shift(1) ffill onto sub-daily bars (M). Reuse triple-barrier/WF/purge/metrics/meta as-is.
out_of_scope: Volume bars, dollar bars, VIB, DIB (degenerate/infeasible for spot-gold CFD with tick-count-only volume). Microstructure features needing real volume (Kyle/VPIN) until/unless real volume exists.

history.reason: investigation: tick/TIB bars only genuine option for XAU CFD; shallow tick history caveat

data-source finding: use Dukascopy XAUUSD tick (free, ~20y, quote-based) for DEEP tick/TIB history instead of MT5-demo (weeks-months) -- removes the fold-starvation caveat for tick/TIB bars. Real volume still requires GC/GLD (B0147).
feat(features): B0134 fractionally-differentiated log-price (FFD, AFML Ch5) + expert-guided backlog
